summary: I re-ran the guide-alignment critique for iteration 10. I read both design guides (`guides/backend-guide.md`, `guides/ui-style-guide.md`) and the full Dashboard diff against `main` — the query objects (`app/queries/dashboard/*`), the `Dashboard::Broadcast` service and its wiring into `Complete`/`Sweep`/`Approve`/`BroadcastCard`/`BroadcastColumn`/`ManagerTick`/`Finalize`, the thin `HomeController`/`PhasesController` changes, the `home/*` view partials, `status_helper#status_badge`, `define_helper`, and the Minitest suites. The architecture is strongly guide-aligned: business logic lives in query-object POROs, controllers stay thin and branch only on `result.success?`/response format, broadcasts fire from services after commit and target the smallest DOM unit over a per-user stream, the shared `status_badge` is the single semantic-color source, and both genuinely new patterns (the presentation-boundary rescue and the cross-pipeline per-user stream) were added to the guides in the same PR exactly as CLAUDE.md requires — so there are no blockers or majors. Since only `input.json`'s iteration counter changed (no code moved), I re-verified rather than assumed, confirmed the six previously-identified **minor** deviations still hold in the current tree (off-scale red/green status shades, a hand-rolled attention badge that bypasses `status_badge`, artifact-selection/parse logic in a view helper, empty states missing the mandated icon+action, fleet-panel polling instead of stream-first without a sanctioning guide note, and off-scale spacing values), refreshed the summary to reflect the iteration-10 re-verification, and left the verdict at **needs_work** with each finding citing its specific guide rule and line.
